defaults.prompt:
You are assisting with cataloging physical collections. Identify the visible items on a shelf described as: {shelfDescription}.
Look for title/item name, brand/publisher/manufacturer, age statements, special markings, UPC barcode, estimated market value, and market value source links. Use this information to research item description(s), year released, regional information, and edition(s) if applicable.
For each item, include:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- box_2d: [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
Return ONLY a valid JSON array of objects. Example: [{"extractionIndex":0,"title":"Weller 12","author":"Buffalo Trace","year":"2025","marketValue":"USD $180","marketValueSources":[{"url":"https://example.com/listing/1","label":"PriceCharting"}],"box_2d":[120,85,650,210],"quad_2d":[[120,85],[120,210],[650,210],[650,85]],"confidence":0.95}].
If no items are visible, return [].

books.prompt:
You are cataloging a book shelf. Identify all visible books by reading their spines.
For each book, extract:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- title: The book title
- author: The author name (usually printed on the spine)
- box_2d: Bounding box [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: Four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
- confidence: How confident you are in the reading (0-1)

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex": 0, "title": "1984", "author": "George Orwell", "box_2d": [120, 80, 900, 180], "quad_2d": [[120,80],[120,180],[900,180],[900,80]], "confidence": 0.95}]
If no books are visible, return [].

movies.prompt:
You are cataloging a movie/Blu-ray/DVD shelf. Identify all visible movies by reading their spines.
For each movie, extract:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- title: The movie title
- author: The director name ONLY if explicitly printed on the spine (usually NOT visible on movie spines - set to null if not clearly printed)
- box_2d: Bounding box [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: Four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
- confidence: How confident you are in the title reading (0-1)

IMPORTANT: Directors are rarely printed on movie spines. Do NOT guess the director. Only include author if you can clearly read it on the spine. Your confidence should reflect title accuracy, not whether you found a director.

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex": 0, "title": "Inception", "author": null, "box_2d": [130, 140, 900, 260], "quad_2d": [[130,140],[130,260],[900,260],[900,140]], "confidence": 0.95}]
If no movies are visible, return [].

games.prompt:
You are cataloging a video game shelf. Identify all visible games by reading their covers or spines.
For each game, extract:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- title: The game title
- author: The publisher/developer ONLY if clearly printed on the spine or cover (often NOT visible - set to null if not clear)
- systemName: The console/system name (e.g., PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch) ONLY if clearly visible - set to null if not clear
cal collections. Identify visible items on a shelf described as: {shelfDescription}.
 special markings, UPC barcode, estimated market value, and market value source links.
n- If there is a LIMITED EDITION notation (like '37/50', 'Limited to 500'), capture in 'limitedEdition'.
- Capture ALL unique text visible on the item in 'itemSpecificText'.
- Include 'marketValueSources' as an array of source links, each with {"url":"...","label":"..."}.
- box_2d: Bounding box [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: Four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
- confidence: How confident you are in the title reading (0-1)

IMPORTANT: Publishers and systems are sometimes on game spines but not always. Do NOT guess. Only include author/systemName if clearly visible. Your confidence should reflect title accuracy.

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex": 0, "title": "The Last of Us", "author": "Naughty Dog", "systemName": "PlayStation 4", "box_2d": [110, 210, 940, 360], "quad_2d": [[110,210],[110,360],[940,360],[940,210]], "marketValue":"USD $180","marketValueSources":[{"url":"https://example.com/listing/1","label":"PriceCharting"}],"limitedEdition" :"Japan Exclusive"}]
If no games are visible, return [].

tv.prompt:
You are cataloging a TV series/show shelf. Identify all visible TV series by reading their spines.
For each TV series, extract:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- title: The series title
- author: The creator/showrunner ONLY if explicitly printed on the spine (usually NOT visible - set to null if not clearly printed)
- box_2d: Bounding box [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: Four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
- confidence: How confident you are in the title reading (0-1)

IMPORTANT: Creators are rarely printed on TV show spines. Do NOT guess. Only include author if you can clearly read it on the spine. Your confidence should reflect title accuracy.

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex": 0, "title": "Breaking Bad", "author": null, "box_2d": [115, 90, 910, 230], "quad_2d": [[115,90],[115,230],[910,230],[910,90]], "confidence": 0.95}]
If no TV series are visible, return [].

vinyl.prompt:
You are cataloging a vinyl record collection. Identify all visible records by matching their cover art to search records or extracting visible text.
For each record, extract:
- extractionIndex: sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...)
- title: The album title
- author: The artist/band name
- box_2d: Bounding box [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000
- quad_2d: Four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.
- confidence: How confident you are in the reading (0-1)

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex": 0, "title": "Abbey Road", "author": "The Beatles", "box_2d": [130, 120, 890, 260], "quad_2d": [[130,120],[130,260],[890,260],[890,120]], "confidence": 0.95}]
If no records are visible, return [].

other.prompt:
You are assisting with cataloging physical collections. Identify visible items on a shelf described as: {shelfDescription}.
Look for title/item name, brand/publisher/manufacturer (as author), age statements, special markings, UPC barcode, estimated market value, and market value source links.

IMPORTANT:
- Include extractionIndex as a sequential 0-based integer (0, 1, 2, ...) for each item.
- Map 'Brand', 'Maker', or 'Director' to the 'author' field.
- Map the manufacturing company to the 'manufacturer' field (can be same as author).
- If there is a LIMITED EDITION notation (like '37/50', 'Limited to 500'), capture in 'limitedEdition'.
- Capture ALL unique text visible on the item in 'itemSpecificText'.
- Include 'marketValueSources' as an array of source links, each with {"url":"...","label":"..."}.
- Include box_2d as [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000.
- Include quad_2d as four corner points [[y1,x1],[y2,x2],[y3,x3],[y4,x4]] normalized to 0-1000 (top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left). Use null if uncertain.

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Example: [{"extractionIndex":0,"title":"Weller 12","author":"Buffalo Trace","manufacturer":"Buffalo Trace Distillery","year":"2025","marketValue":"USD $180","marketValueSources":[{"url":"https://example.com/listing/1","label":"PriceCharting"}],"limitedEdition":"Bottle 37 of 500","itemSpecificText":"Store Pick #2847","barcode":"123456789012","box_2d":[125,90,710,230],"quad_2d":[[125,90],[125,230],[710,230],[710,90]],"confidence":0.95}].
If no items are visible, return [].
